subject: English
source: National Curriculum Framework for School Education (NCF-SE), Section 2.4.1.3 — Language 1 (R1)

CG-1.label: Advanced Communication: Writing, Media and Technology
CG-1.description: Uses language for effective communication through writing various forms (essays, letters, articles, discussions, interviews, public speeches) and for new media (email, audio, and visual material)

competency_codes.C-1.1: Uses language appropriate to social context, expresses agreement and disagreement with reasons, and arrives at conclusions through discussion and debate
competency_codes.C-1.2: Writes in different styles (narrative, descriptive, expository, persuasive) from their own experiences and experiences of others
competency_codes.C-1.3: Writes for real-life situations (invitations, speeches, condolence messages, notices, creative slogans, advertisements) and for school newsletter/magazine/journal
competency_codes.C-1.4: Scripts to inform and communicate ideas effectively with the use of technology

CG-2.label: Literary Aesthetics: Genre, Style and Creative Composition
CG-2.description: Develops an appreciation of the aesthetics in different genres (humour, suspense, tragedy) through analysis of style (narrative, descriptive, expository, persuasive) and employs these elements in their writing

competency_codes.C-2.1: Describes characteristics of works of literature from different time periods (such as early, medieval, contemporary)
competency_codes.C-2.2: Analyses a literary text by close reading, critiquing form and style, and interpreting possible meanings
competency_codes.C-2.3: Composes literary texts by using appropriate literary devices

CG-3.label: Critical Reasoning and Argumentation
CG-3.description: Uses language to develop reasoning and argumentation skills by engaging with a variety of audio and written material

competency_codes.C-3.1: Analyses and evaluates the different audio and written material
competency_codes.C-3.2: Argues with proper rationale by carefully evaluating premises

CG-4.label: Indian Literary Heritage and Multilingualism
CG-4.description: Appreciates literary and cultural heritage in and related to the language and the richness of Indian languages

competency_codes.C-4.1: Recognises the multilingual nature of Indian society and richness of its literary work through reading texts and watching content of different genres
competency_codes.C-4.2: Appreciates the richness of culture and heritage in the different works of regional language literature and their connections
competency_codes.C-4.3: Shows an understanding of the role of language in the formation of our identities and culture
competency_codes.C-4.4: Demonstrates a basic knowledge of the commonalities among some of the major Indian languages, such as their common phonetic and scientifically arranged alphabets and scripts, common grammatical structures, origins and sources of vocabularies from Sanskrit and other classical languages, as well as their rich inter-influences and differences
competency_codes.C-4.5: Demonstrates a basic knowledge of which languages are spoken in which geographical areas, a sense of the nature and structure of tribal languages, and becomes familiar with a few useful words and phrases and works of literature from a few Indian languages from across the country
